Command.Name: __default_command
Command.Description: Generates dependency graphs for .NET projects and NuGet packages.

Argument.Name: SOURCE
Argument.Description: The source of the graph Can be either * the name of a NuGet package, optionally with a specific version, e.g. [b]Newtonsoft.Json/13.0.3[/] * a .NET project file (csproj/fsproj/vbproj) * a directory containing a single .NET project

Option.Description: The direction of the dependency graph, possible values are [b]LeftToRight[/] (recommended for large graphs) and [b]TopToBottom[/] (good for small graphs)

The format to use when the [b]--output[/] option is not specified Use [b]mmd[/] or [b]mermaid[/] for Mermaid Live Editor https://mermaid.live Use [b]dot[/], [b]gv[/] or [b]graphviz[/] for Edotor https://edotor.net See https://github.com/0xced/nugraph/#output for even more supported formats

The target framework to consider when building the dependency graph See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/frameworks#supported-target-frameworks for the list of supported target frameworks
Packages to ignore in the dependency graph, may be used multiple times and supports * wildcards, e.g. [b]--ignore "System.*"[/]
Include package versions in the dependency graph nodes, e.g. [b]Serilog/4.3.0[/] instead of [b]Serilog[/]
The NuGet operations log level, possible values are [b]Debug[/], [b]Verbose[/], [b]Information[/], [b]Minimal[/], [b]Warning[/] and [b]Error[/]
Remove clickable links from the the dependency graph Can be useful to reduce the size of the graph if it becomes too large and Mermaid Live Editor is returning "Maximum text size in diagram exceeded"

The path to the dependency graph output file A Mermaid diagram is written if the file name ends with either [b].mmd[/] or [b].mermaid[/], otherwise a Graphviz diagram is written If not specified, the default web browser is used to render the dependency graph using an online service See also the [b]--format[/] and [b]--url[/] options
The target runtime to consider when building the dependency graph See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/rid-catalog#known-rids for the list of known runtime identifiers
The title of the dependency graph

Defines what to do when the [b]--output[/] option is not specified Use [b]open[/] (the default) for rendering the dependency graph using an online service or [b]print[/] for printing the graph URL on the standard output without opening the browser Both options can be combined with a comma, i.e. [b]open,print[/] to both open the URL and print it
